case_title: DAVID BOATRIGHT VS. JOHNSON & JOHNSON SERVICES, INC. et al

docket_entries.description: PRODUCTS LIABILITY, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BOATRIGHT, DAVID AS TO DEFENDANT JOHNSON & JOHNSON SERVICES, INC. JOHNSON & JOHNSON INC. DEPUY ORTHOPAEDICS, INC. DEPUY, INC VAIL CONSULTING, LLC THOMAS VAIL, MD THOMAS SCHMALZRIED MD SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR JUL-01-2011 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  APR-04-2011 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         JUN-16-2011 COMPLEX LITIGATION ASSIGNMENT REQUESTED BY FILING PARTIES; FEE INCLUDED IN FILING FEE

LAW AND MOTION, 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: VERIFIED APPLICATION OF ROBERT J. PATTERSON TO APPEAR COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR THE PLAINTIFF DAVID BOATRIGHT IS TAKEN OFF-CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF REFILING THE MOTION. AFTER REVIEWING THE LETTER OF APRIL 8, 2011 THAT WAS DROPPED OFF IN THE DEPARTMENT'S OUTBOX, THE COURT FINDS THAT IT IS NOT A VALID PROOF OF SERVICE INDICATIVE OF SUFFICIENT NOTICE UNDER C C P 1005 (B). THE LETTER IS MERELY AN AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES OF THE CONTINUATION OF THE HEARING DATE AND DOES NOT INDICATE THAT ALL PARTIES HAVE RECEIVED PROPER NOTICE FOR THE HEARING DATE OR WERE AGREEING TO THE RELIEF. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

RULING:
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF DAVID BOATRIGHT'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. NO OPPOSITION FILED. MOVING PARTY PROVIDED THE COURT AT THE HEARING WITH A VALID PROOF OF SERVICE INDICATIVE OF SUFFICIENT NOTICE OF HEARING. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE

MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
VERIFIED APPLICATION OF ROBERT J. PATTERSON TO APPEAR COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE FOR THE PLAINTIFF DAVID BOATRIGHT IS TAKEN OFF-CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF REFILING THE MOTION. AFTER REVIEWING THE LETTER OF APRIL 8, 2011 THAT WAS DROPPED OFF IN THE DEPARTMENT'S OUTBOX, THE COURT FINDS THAT IT IS NOT A VALID PROOF OF SERVICE INDICATIVE OF SUFFICIENT NOTICE UNDER C C P 1005 (B). THE LETTER IS MERELY AN AGREEMENT OF THE PARTIES OF THE CONTINUATION OF THE HEARING DATE AND DOES NOT INDICATE THAT ALL PARTIES HAVE RECEIVED PROPER NOTICE FOR THE HEARING DATE OR WERE AGREEING TO THE RELIEF. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
